evals.prompt: Check this claim from arXiv paper 2603.15066 (Multi-Mode Pneumatic Artificial Muscles): 'The linear IN-FOAM achieves a maximum contraction ratio of ~43%'. Fetch Figure 2 from https://arxiv.org/html/2603.15066v1 and verify whether the figure supports this specific value for IN-FOAM.
evals.expected_output: MATCH verdict. Agent fetches the figure from arXiv HTML, reads the contraction ratio axis for IN-FOAM, reports the read value (~43%), compares against claim, and produces a structured verification card. Every number must come from the actual figure, not recalled from training.
expectations: Figure was actually fetched from the arXiv URL — not described from memory
Output specifies which axis was read and what scale it uses
Read value for IN-FOAM is reported with how it was extracted from the figure
Verdict field is present (MATCH, APPROXIMATE, MISMATCH, or UNREADABLE)
Output does not guess values without stating they came from the figure

evals.prompt: Verify this claim from arXiv paper 2603.15066: 'The IN-FOAM achieves a maximum contraction ratio of 28%'. Fetch Figure 2 from https://arxiv.org/html/2603.15066v1 and check if this is correct.
evals.expected_output: MISMATCH verdict. The 28% value belongs to Pouch Motor, not IN-FOAM. The skill should read the figure, identify the IN-FOAM curve/bar, read its actual value (~43%), and flag that the claimed 28% does not match what the figure shows for IN-FOAM. This is the most important eval — baseline will likely just describe the figure without catching the specific wrong-actor mismatch.
expectations: Figure was fetched from arXiv URL
Skill identifies which data series corresponds to IN-FOAM specifically (not just any bar)
Skill reads IN-FOAM value as different from the claimed 28%
Output states what value the figure actually shows for IN-FOAM and what 28% corresponds to if identifiable

evals.prompt: Check this claim from arXiv paper 2604.24140: 'The first resonance mode appears at approximately 390 Hz for the 100 μm membrane with 10% prestrain'. Fetch Figure 9 from https://arxiv.org/html/2604.24140v1 and verify.
evals.expected_output: MATCH or APPROXIMATE verdict. Agent fetches Figure 9, reads the frequency axis, identifies the first resonance peak for the 100μm/10% prestrain condition, and reports the read value. If the figure shows ~390 Hz, MATCH. If close but axis precision limits exact reading, APPROXIMATE with stated uncertainty.
expectations: Figure was fetched from arXiv URL
Agent correctly identifies what 'first resonance mode' means in the context of the figure (first peak in frequency spectrum)
Agent reports the frequency value read from the figure with units (Hz)
Verdict appropriately reflects match quality (MATCH or APPROXIMATE with uncertainty noted)
If multiple conditions are shown, agent identifies the 100μm/10% prestrain curve specifically
